title: The Movie Star Ranking: 35 Over 35, Part II
format: Sean and Amanda count down the back half of their 35-over-35 movie star ranking, sort through who just missed, and take a call from Bobby doing his Diane Lane voice to remind them they forgot Mark Wahlberg.

segments.heading: Catching Up
segments.note: feedback on the 35-under-35 list, and who they forgot

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: Jesse Plemons's current film, cited as proof the under-35 list is aging well.

segments.heading: Who's Off the List
segments.note: ineligible names, the middle-aged-comedy-star die-off, and the icons who don't qualify

films.title: Coming to America
films.note: Eddie Murphy barely qualifies given the imminent release of its sequel.

films.title: Dunkirk
films.note: One of three Tom Hardy films in his qualifying window; he's barely onscreen in it.

films.title: Capone
films.note: The Hardy film Sean calls 'no bueno.'

films.title: The House
films.note: Will Ferrell's last good comedy, per Sean, and the start of the run that keeps him off the list.

films.title: Eurovision Song Contest: The Story of Fire Saga
films.note: Ferrell's most recent, which neither host connected with.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: Cited when weighing whether Robert De Niro and Al Pacino belong on the list.

films.title: The War with Grandpa
films.note: Neither host has seen it; floated as a bit for a future episode.

films.title: Ma Rainey's Black Bottom
films.note: Cited in discussing where the late Chadwick Boseman would have ranked.

films.title: Good Will Hunting
films.note: No. 20 · Ben Affleck, misheard on air as 'a good while hunting': the film that won him and Matt Damon their screenplay Oscar.

films.title: Just Mercy
films.note: No. 19 · Jamie Foxx, cited as recent, strong work.

films.title: Project Power
films.note: The Netflix movie Sean calls not very effective, despite the audience it found.

films.title: Baby Driver
films.note: Cited alongside Just Mercy as evidence Foxx belongs on the list.

films.title: All-Star Weekend
films.note: Foxx's unreleased directorial effort, starring Robert Downey Jr., set during NBA All-Star Weekend.

films.title: Happy Gilmore
films.note: No. 18 · Adam Sandler, its 25th anniversary marked days earlier when he recreated the ball-crushing shot on Twitter.

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: Cited as proof Sandler can thread prestige and Netflix comedy.

films.title: Murder Mystery
films.note: Sandler and Jennifer Aniston's Netflix comedy; a sequel is discussed but unconfirmed.

films.title: Bridesmaids
films.note: No. 17 · Melissa McCarthy, the film that made her a movie star.

films.title: Spy
films.note: One of the studio comedies both hosts still like from her first run.

films.title: Can You Ever Forgive Me?
films.note: Earned McCarthy an Oscar nomination.

films.title: The Little Mermaid
films.note: McCarthy was set to voice Ursula in Disney's live-action remake.

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: No. 16 · Bradley Cooper, the film that made the hosts big supporters of his.

films.title: The Mule
films.note: Cooper's qualifying role, playing a federal agent tailing Clint Eastwood's character.

films.title: War Dogs
films.note: Directed by Todd Phillips, whose later collaboration with Cooper on Joker got them both producing Oscar nominations.

films.title: American Sniper
films.note: Referenced for the scene where Cooper cradles a fake baby doll: 'that's cinema,' per Amanda.

films.title: Nightmare Alley
films.note: Guillermo del Toro's upcoming film, also starring Cate Blanchett.

films.title: Licorice Pizza
films.note: Then known by its working title, Soggy Bottom; Paul Thomas Anderson's next film, also with Cooper.

films.title: Suicide Squad
films.note: No. 15 · Viola Davis's first go with this cast of characters.

films.title: The Suicide Squad
films.note: James Gunn's unrelated film with an overlapping cast, which briefly confuses Amanda.

films.title: Ma Rainey's Black Bottom
films.note: Expected to earn Davis her fourth Oscar nomination.

films.title: Let Them All Talk
films.note: Steven Soderbergh's film, cited alongside his history of casting Davis early in her career.

films.title: Snakes on a Plane
films.note: No. 14 · Samuel L. Jackson, cited as evidence he's 'not picky in the good way,' and that the movie is actually good.

films.title: Aladdin
films.note: No. 12 · Will Smith, in the live-action remake, punctuated for Amanda by the DJ Khaled 'Friend Like Me' remix.

films.title: Bad Boys for Life
films.note: 2020's biggest box-office hit, even accounting for the pandemic.

films.title: King Richard
films.note: Smith's upcoming Serena and Venus Williams biopic, seen as a real Oscar bid.

films.title: Avengers: Infinity War
films.note: No. 11 · Robert Downey Jr., part of the run that made him the MCU's central figure.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: His character's death closes the film: 'the emotional hammer,' per Sean.

films.title: Dolittle
films.note: A poor film both hosts saw together the first week of January 2020.

films.title: Sully
films.note: No. 10 · one of the last five years of Tom Hanks films the hosts cite as proof he's still hit-making.

films.title: A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood
Thor: Ragnarok
films.note: No. 9 · Cate Blanchett, cited as evidence she goes all-in even in franchise roles.

films.title: Carol
films.note: One of the smaller films Amanda assumes Blanchett makes for love rather than money.

films.title: Guillermo del Toro's Pinocchio
films.note: Del Toro's stop-motion version, then upcoming.

films.title: Don't Look Up
films.note: Adam McKay's film, mistranscribed on air as an instruction rather than a title. Also stars DiCaprio, at No. 1.

films.title: Fast & Furious Presents: Hobbs & Shaw
films.note: No. 8 · Dwayne Johnson, the film behind Amanda's story about a bomb scare and his 20-minute improvised crowd work.

films.title: Fast Five
films.note: The only Johnson movie Amanda claims to remember the plot of.

films.title: Red Notice
films.note: His then-upcoming film, also starring Ryan Reynolds and Gal Gadot.

films.title: Be Cool
films.note: The Get Shorty sequel; Johnson was 'really really funny in it,' per Sean, despite the movie's reputation.

films.title: Something's Gotta Give
films.note: No. 7 · Keanu Reeves, Amanda's favorite performance of his.

films.title: Parenthood
films.note: Sean's pick: 'incredibly sweet.'

films.title: Always Be My Maybe
films.note: His famous cameo.

films.title: Bill & Ted Face the Music
films.note: One of the bigger movies to come out of quarantine.

films.title: The Lake House
films.note: The tail end of a career lull that John Wick reversed.

films.title: Let Them All Talk
films.note: No. 6 · Meryl Streep, Soderbergh's more experimental register for her, versus her broader 'Mamma Mia-ish' work.

films.title: Florence Foster Jenkins
films.note: One of her more recent Oscar nominations.

films.title: The Bridges of Madison County
films.note: Together with Postcards, bookends the longest previous gap between Streep Oscar nominations.

segments.heading: Bobby Calls In as Diane Lane

films.title: The Perfect Storm
films.note: Diane Lane's character is the bit's jumping-off point: Bobby calls in doing her voice to complain Wahlberg's been forgotten.

films.title: Spenser Confidential
films.note: Cited as proof Wahlberg can still 'open' a streaming movie.

films.title: All the Money in the World
films.note: Wahlberg was paid $1.5 million for reshoots; Michelle Williams was paid a fraction of that.

films.title: Instant Family
films.note: The 'family-friendly' Wahlberg movie Amanda says she doesn't watch.

films.title: Transformers: The Last Knight
films.note: Chris, doing the bit, claims to have seen it six or seven times in theaters.

films.title: Patriots Day
films.note: Cited together with Deepwater Horizon as Wahlberg's based-on-a-true-story run.

films.title: Bullet Train
films.note: No. 5 · Brad Pitt, directed by David Leitch; Sean says he'd watch it no matter who starred.

films.title: Moneyball
films.note: Amanda jokingly warns Sean not to bring it up.

films.title: The Little Things
films.note: No. 4 · Denzel Washington, a mediocre film both hosts enjoyed purely because he's in it.

films.title: The Tragedy of Macbeth
films.note: His upcoming Joel Coen film, referenced on air simply as 'a Macbeth.'

films.title: Mission: Impossible – Dead Reckoning Part One
films.note: No. 3 · Tom Cruise, unreleased at the time and referred to only as Mission: Impossible 7.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: Both delayed films the hosts hope actually come out this year.

films.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
films.note: No. 2 · Charlize Theron, the film Sean says elevated her to a stardom plane 'we've basically never seen for an actress.'

films.title: Long Shot
films.note: A movie both hosts love that almost nobody saw.

films.title: The Old Guard
films.note: A film Theron carries despite not being its sole star.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: No. 1 · Leonardo DiCaprio's lone film of the past five years, and the reason the hosts break their own eligibility rule for him.

films.title: Titanic
films.note: Brought up separately as the DiCaprio movie everyone forgot to mention.

films.title: Don't Look Up
films.note: DiCaprio's next film, also starring Cate Blanchett.

TV: On Becoming a God in Central Florida
Ads: Netflix FIFA World Cup Launch Edition

A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood.title: A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood

All the Money in the World.title: All the Money in the World

Mission: Impossible – Dead Reckoning Par.title: Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Part One
